content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s43681-024-00467-w", "title": "Beyond phase-in: assessing impacts on disinformation of the EU Digital Services Act", "summary": "It provides background on the DSA\u2019s context, mechanisms, timeline, and expected effects on platforms to situate the need for rigorous impact assessment. A detailed legal, technical, psychological, behavioral and ethical critique reveals meaningful gaps in the DSA requiring ongoing regulatory refinement and oversight. Most critically, the paper puts forth an encompassing framework spanning computational analytics, interviews, ethnography, surveys, discourse analysis and mixed methods to rigorously assess the DSA\u2019s multi-dimensional effects on complex factors enabling disinformation proliferation.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Neverthe- less, these opaque systems also have the potential to gener- ate manipulative content, disseminate harmful disinforma- tion, and perpetuate systematic biases on a massive scale without meaningful human oversight or transparency [1, 13, 25, ", "Academic discourse has thus far only provided piecemeal or theoretical perspec- tives on the DSA\u2019s potential [61, 78, 82, 104, 108, 109, 169], rather than rigorously quantifying its multidimen- sional effects on complex factors enabling the phenomeno", "Within, Sect. 2.2 eluci- dates the provisions on AI accountability.", "Critiques of the DSA\u2019s approach towards disinformation are articulated in Sect. 3, encompassing an analysis of the DSA\u2019s regulatory antecedents Sect. 3.1 and leading to a review of the DSA\u2019s persistent gaps in Sect. 3.2.", "At its core, the DSA establishes transparency duties, risk analysis requirements and independent auditing processes for Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) and search engines reaching over 10% of the EU population\u2014around 45 million monthly active use"], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Neverthe- less, these opaque systems also have the potential to gener- ate manipulative content, disseminate harmful dis"}, {"url": "https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5991717", "title": "Future of Telecom and Digital Regulation", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://rucforsk.ruc.dk/ws/files/108529058/DSA_Project__7_.pdf", "title": "Early Lessons from the Digital Services Act in its Effectiveness in Regulating Disinformation", "summary": "By employing a logic model focused on inputs, outputs and early outcomes, we aim to analyse the effectiveness of the DSA in tackling disinformation through a content analysis of relevant documents. These documents comprise the DSA legislative text, risk assessments, transparency reports, and European Commission decisions and press releases on the proceedings against the three platforms. The findings suggest that the DSA is ineffective in regulating disinformation, but positive signs are present in content moderation.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Literature Review.........................................................................................................................6 3.1 Regulating Disinformation.................................................................................", "Introduction Since the US elections in 2016, online disinformation and \u201cfake news\u201d on online platforms have become a growing concern among policymakers, citizens and experts as they arguably have an impact on the electoral system and hence pose a thr", "With the majority of the population now using social media platforms for communication, sharing of information, and news consumption, platforms have not only become \u201csuperspreaders\u201d of disinformation but platform providers are also becoming key gatek", "Thus, digital services have become a new source of risks and challenges, both for society at large and for individuals (Chiarella, 2023, p.33).", "This is leading to increasingly interventionist policies (Cioffi et al., 2022)."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Literature Review......................................................................................................."}]}
